name: Potential Lateral Movement via PowerShell
notes:
This rule detects a PowerShell process spawned as a child or grand child process of commonly abused processes during lateral movement.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Mauricio Velazco, Splunk
SIGMA Title: Possible Lateral Movement PowerShell Spawn
SIGMA Description: The following analytic assists with identifying a PowerShell process spawned as a child or grand child process of commonly abused processes during lateral movement techniques including `services.exe`, `wmiprsve.exe`, `svchost.exe`, `wsmprovhost.exe` and `mmc.exe`. Legitimate Windows features such as the Service Control Manager, Windows Management Instrumentation, Task Scheduler, Windows Remote Management and the DCOM protocol can be abused to start a process on a remote endpoint. Looking for PowerShell spawned out of this processes may reveal a lateral movement attack. Red Teams and adversaries alike may abuse these services during a breach for lateral movement and remote code execution.


Confidence: HIGH

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches LOWER("Parent Process Full Full Name") in ('wmiprvse.exe', 'services.exe', 'svchost.exe', 'wsmprovhost.exe', 'mmc.exe') AQL filter query

when the event matches LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE 'powershell.exe' OR (LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE 'cmd.exe' AND (LOWER("Command") LIKE '%powershell.exe%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%pwsh.exe%')) OR LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE 'pwsh.exe' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Potential Lateral Movement via PowerShell on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches LOWER("Parent Process Full Full Name") in ('wmiprvse.exe', 'services.exe', 'svchost.exe', 'wsmprovhost.exe', 'mmc.exe') AQL filter query

AND when the event matches LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE 'powershell.exe' OR (LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE 'cmd.exe' AND (LOWER("Command") LIKE '%powershell.exe%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%pwsh.exe%')) OR LOWER("Process Full Full Name") LIKE 'pwsh.exe' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Potential Lateral Movement via PowerShell
Event description: Detects a PowerShell process spawned as a child or grand child process of commonly abused processes during lateral movement.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
